operations.action: analyzed repository structure, graph nodes, service runtime, evaluation pipeline, schemas, and policy/lookup assets
operations.reason: A complete system-state record required direct inspection of the implemented pipeline, storage model, schema surfaces, and execution entrypoints.

operations.action: created docs/SITREC-BASELINE-2026-04-18.md
operations.reason: The requested deliverable was a rehydratable baseline document capturing current repository state in a single readable artifact.

payload.description: Create a fine-tuning dataset for ICD mapping structured output

operations.action: generated fine_tuning/icd_mapping_dataset.jsonl with 100 chat fine-tuning examples using a fixed system prompt and strict JSON assistant outputs
operations.reason: The requested artifact required a standalone dataset covering clear mappings, ambiguous cases, unmappable inputs, and multi-diagnosis examples while staying within the specified output schema.

operations.action: validated line count, JSON parsing, assistant-output schema conformance, and allowed status values for every dataset row
operations.reason: Verification was necessary to ensure the file satisfied the required schema exactly and contained no extra keys or invalid status values.

payload.description: Attempt to submit an external fine-tuning job using the existing ICD mapping dataset

operations.action: verified the dataset path, checked for API credentials and client availability, and submitted the training file to the external provider
operations.reason: Starting a fine-tuning job required confirming the local dataset and environment prerequisites before making the provider API request.

operations.action: stopped after the provider rejected the file upload with an authentication error and did not create fine_tuning/fine_tune_job.json
operations.reason: The request specified stopping on missing or unusable credentials, and the provider returned a direct invalid API key response during file upload.

payload.description: Diagnose external fine-tuning authentication failure and persist a redacted auth diagnostic artifact

operations.action: checked repo-local .env presence, compared the process environment key to the .env value without exposing the full secret, and inspected whether the fine-tuning execution path explicitly loaded dotenv
operations.reason: Identifying the credential source required establishing whether the failing request used the process environment, the repo-local .env file, or an explicit dotenv load path.

operations.action: performed a minimal authenticated provider probe with the process environment credential and wrote fine_tuning/auth_diagnostic.json with redacted findings
operations.reason: A direct provider response was needed to distinguish an invalid process credential from formatting issues and to persist a factual diagnostic artifact without altering application logic.

payload.description: Submit an external fine-tuning job using the repo-local .env credential for this execution only

operations.action: loaded OPENAI_API_KEY directly from the repo-local .env into the submission process, verified the dataset file, and checked the credential shape in memory without printing or persisting the secret
operations.reason: The request required using the repo-local credential source for one execution while avoiding any mutation of the stored key and avoiding disclosure of the full secret.

operations.action: submitted the dataset to OpenAI with base model gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18 and wrote fine_tuning/fine_tune_job.json with the returned job facts
operations.reason: Persisting the provider-returned identifiers and job metadata was necessary to capture the accepted fine-tuning submission without integrating the model into application logic.

payload.description: Generate baseline-versus-fine-tune sample ICD mapping outputs and persist a comparison artifact

operations.action: read fine_tuning/fine_tune_job.json, queried the provider for the latest job state, and determined that the fine-tuning job was running with no callable fine-tuned model identifier yet available
operations.reason: The task required checking whether a real fine-tuned model could be invoked before attempting comparison outputs, and the provider job state was the authoritative source for that fact.

operations.action: generated baseline model outputs for five diagnosis-level test inputs and wrote fine_tuning/sample_outputs.json with null fine_tuned_output values because the fine-tuned model was still unavailable
operations.reason: The requested artifact required factual sample comparisons, and the instructions required preserving partial results instead of fabricating fine-tuned outputs when the model was not yet callable.

payload.description: Refresh the external fine-tuning job state and populate fine-tuned sample outputs only if a callable model becomes available

operations.action: loaded OPENAI_API_KEY directly from the repo-local .env for this execution, queried the provider for the latest fine-tuning job state, and updated fine_tuning/fine_tune_job.json with the returned job facts
operations.reason: The task required an authoritative provider refresh of the recorded fine-tuning job before deciding whether fine-tuned inference was possible.

operations.action: left the existing five sample inputs and baseline outputs unchanged and stopped without inference because the job remained running and no callable fine_tuned_model identifier was available
operations.reason: The instructions required preserving the existing comparison artifact and avoiding fabricated fine-tuned outputs until the provider exposed a real callable model identifier.

payload.description: Monitor the external fine-tuning job for up to five minutes and complete sample output generation only if the fine-tuned model becomes callable

operations.action: loaded OPENAI_API_KEY directly from the repo-local .env for this execution, polled the provider every 20 seconds for five minutes, and updated fine_tuning/fine_tune_job.json with the latest observed job state
operations.reason: The task required bounded monitoring of the provider job to detect a terminal successful state without running indefinitely and to preserve the latest authoritative job facts.

operations.action: stopped at the five-minute limit with job_status still running and no callable fine_tuned_model identifier, leaving the existing five sample inputs and baseline outputs unchanged in fine_tuning/sample_outputs.json
operations.reason: The instructions required ending at the timeout boundary and avoiding fabricated or premature fine-tuned outputs when the provider did not expose a callable model within the allowed polling window.

payload.description: Completed Phase 1A fine-tuning artifact handling within bounded polling and single recovery constraints.

operations.action: poll_existing_job
operations.reason: bounded status check for ftjob-SyqBeIUHDFGZ0W4mLSwzEJxs

operations.action: generate_fine_tuned_outputs
operations.reason: usable model artifact available: ft:gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18:personal::DW474vx2

payload.description: Created a standalone ICD evaluation dataset with deterministic expected outputs and non-overlapping prompt phrasing for offline scoring.

operations.action: wrote evaluation/icd_eval_cases.json with 24 schema-conforming cases spanning OK, REVIEW, ERROR, multi-diagnosis, and noisy-input coverage
operations.reason: A deterministic evaluation set was required for ICD mapping assessment without reusing the fine-tuning prompt strings or changing any existing pipeline components.

payload.description: Implemented a standalone ICD evaluation runner that invokes baseline and fine-tuned models, normalizes outputs, computes aggregate metrics, and classifies failures without persisting evaluation results.

operations.action: wrote evaluation/run_icd_eval.py with dataset loading, fine-tuned model loading, three-run per model execution, deterministic output comparison, aggregate metrics, and failure taxonomy classification
operations.reason: A dedicated evaluation script was required to measure baseline versus fine-tuned ICD mapping behavior through external API inference while keeping evaluation logic isolated from the production pipeline and existing schemas.

payload.description: Executed the standalone ICD evaluation runner, persisted baseline and fine-tuned result artifacts, and wrote an aggregate summary comparison for both models.

operations.action: ran evaluation/run_icd_eval.py logic and wrote evaluation/icd_eval_results_baseline.json, evaluation/icd_eval_results_finetuned.json, and evaluation/icd_eval_summary.json
operations.reason: The evaluation step required live model inference for both baseline and fine-tuned ICD mapping, numeric metric capture, aggregated error taxonomy counts, and persisted comparison artifacts without changing datasets or pipeline behavior.

payload.description: Implemented a non-persisting wrapper that executes the full governed MED-SCRIBE pipeline once and returns governance-facing decision artifacts.

operations.action: wrote evaluation/run_governed_pipeline.py with input normalization, direct graph execution, and safe extraction of final status, escalation flag, critic scores, and raw pipeline state
operations.reason: A callable evaluation hook was required to run the existing governed pipeline end-to-end without altering stage logic, governance rules, schemas, service behavior, or runtime storage.

payload.description: Executed the governed MED-SCRIBE pipeline three times per evaluation case and persisted per-case governed results for downstream metric computation.

operations.action: loaded evaluation/icd_eval_cases.json, invoked evaluation/run_governed_pipeline.py three times for each case input, and wrote evaluation/icd_eval_results_governed.json with preserved inputs and full governed run payloads
operations.reason: Downstream governed metric computation required a persisted, repeated-run evaluation artifact that matches the Phase 1B dataset size and retains the raw governed outputs without changing existing baseline or fine-tuned result files.

payload.description: Computed governance metrics from the persisted governed evaluation artifact and wrote a unified full-system comparison package using the existing baseline and fine-tuned summaries as source of truth.

operations.action: read the persisted baseline, fine-tuned, governed, and summary artifacts; computed policy compliance, decision stability, escalation rate, and override rate from governed runs; updated evaluation/icd_eval_results_governed.json with top-level metrics; and wrote evaluation/full_system_comparison.json with copied baseline and fine-tuned values plus governed metrics
operations.reason: The comparison packaging step required governance-level metrics derived from the existing governed artifact and a unified cross-stage summary without recomputing prior baseline or fine-tuned metrics or altering any evaluation inputs or pipeline logic.

payload.description: Created an expanded ICD evaluation dataset with balanced deterministic case coverage for scale validation while preserving the Phase 1B schema.

operations.action: wrote evaluation/icd_eval_scale_cases.json with 120 schema-conforming cases spanning OK, REVIEW, ERROR, multi-diagnosis, and noisy-input scenarios without reusing the original evaluation cases verbatim
operations.reason: Scale validation required a larger deterministic evaluation corpus with the same schema as the Phase 1B dataset, balanced case-type coverage, and distinct inputs so system behavior can be tested at higher volume without changing pipeline logic.

payload.description: Executed the expanded ICD scale evaluation once per input across baseline, fine-tuned, and governed systems, measured latency, and wrote the aggregated scale summary artifact.

operations.action: loaded evaluation/icd_eval_scale_cases.json, invoked the baseline model once per case, invoked the fine-tuned model once per case, invoked the governed pipeline once per case, computed generation and performance metrics for each system, and wrote evaluation/icd_eval_scale_summary.json
operations.reason: Scaled comparison required one pass per system over the expanded dataset with per-run latency capture, numeric aggregate metrics, and a single summary artifact without modifying datasets, pipeline logic, governance logic, or prior Phase 1B metrics.

payload.description: Created a bounded v2 ICD fine-tuning dataset by preserving the v1 base rows and appending targeted semantic-correction examples for specificity and OK-versus-REVIEW behavior.

operations.action: read fine_tuning/icd_mapping_dataset.jsonl and the evaluation artifacts, then wrote fine_tuning/icd_mapping_dataset_v2.jsonl with the original v1 rows plus 30 additional chat-format examples targeting hypertension specificity, dehydration specificity, mixed-status combinations, and ambiguity handling
operations.reason: The next fine-tuning iteration required a narrow dataset refinement focused on the dominant structurally valid but semantically incorrect outputs, especially exact-code selection and overconfident OK classifications, while preserving the existing JSONL chat structure and keeping the scope bounded to one additive v2 dataset update.

payload.description: Submitted a single second-round ICD fine-tuning job using the v2 dataset and persisted the initial provider job state without polling for completion.

operations.action: loaded the repo-local API key, uploaded fine_tuning/icd_mapping_dataset_v2.jsonl, created one fine-tuning job on the same gpt-4o-mini model family as v1, and wrote fine_tuning/fine_tune_job_v2.json with the initial job metadata
operations.reason: The next bounded fine-tuning step required exactly one additional external API job submission using the refined v2 dataset while preserving pipeline code, governance logic, and schema files and avoiding indefinite polling.

payload.description: Monitored the v2 fine-tuning job within the bounded window, updated the v2 job artifact with the latest provider state, and generated the three-way sample comparison only if the new fine-tuned model became callable.

operations.action: polled the v2 fine-tuning job using the repo-local API key and updated fine_tuning/fine_tune_job_v2.json with the latest provider facts
operations.reason: Phase 4 Step B3 required bounded follow-through on the existing v2 fine-tuning job so the model could be used only if the provider exposed a callable fine-tuned model within the allowed wait window.

payload.description: Performed a single provider state check for the v2 fine-tuning job, refreshed the persisted v2 job metadata, and generated the three-way sample comparison only if the new model was already callable.

operations.action: queried the provider once for the current v2 fine-tuning job state using the repo-local API key and updated fine_tuning/fine_tune_job_v2.json with the returned facts
operations.reason: This execution was required to advance the v2 rollout idempotently with a single provider state check so the job record reflects the latest status without any polling or repeated retries.

payload.description: Monitored the v2 fine-tuning job within the bounded window, updated the v2 job artifact with the latest provider state, and generated the three-way sample comparison only if the new fine-tuned model became callable.

operations.action: polled the v2 fine-tuning job using the repo-local API key and updated fine_tuning/fine_tune_job_v2.json with the latest provider facts
operations.reason: Phase 4 Step B3 required bounded follow-through on the existing v2 fine-tuning job so the model could be used only if the provider exposed a callable fine-tuned model within the allowed wait window.

payload.description: Performed a single provider state check for the v2 fine-tuning job, refreshed the persisted v2 job metadata, and generated the three-way sample comparison only if the new model was already callable.

operations.action: queried the provider once for the current v2 fine-tuning job state using the repo-local API key and updated fine_tuning/fine_tune_job_v2.json with the returned facts
operations.reason: This execution was required to advance the v2 rollout idempotently with a single provider state check so the job record reflects the latest status without any polling or repeated retries.

operations.action: created fine_tuning/sample_outputs_v2.json by preserving the original sample order and copying the existing baseline and v1 outputs while generating fresh v2 outputs from the callable v2 fine-tuned model
operations.reason: The three-way comparison artifact was only permitted when the provider exposed a callable v2 model, and the sample inputs and prior outputs had to remain unchanged while adding schema-compliant v2 outputs.

payload.description: Executed a fresh Phase 1B comparison pass across baseline, fine-tuned v1, fine-tuned v2, and the governed pipeline, then wrote a compact comparison artifact focused on accuracy, schema validity, and governance behavior.

operations.action: loaded evaluation/icd_eval_cases.json, ran one baseline inference per case, ran one fine-tuned v1 inference per case, ran one fine-tuned v2 inference per case, ran one governed pipeline execution per case, computed generation and governance comparison metrics, and wrote evaluation/icd_eval_v2_comparison.json
operations.reason: The Phase 4 comparison step required a fresh, same-dataset measurement of semantic improvement for the new v2 model alongside current baseline, v1, and governed behavior without overwriting any prior evaluation artifacts.

payload.description: Appended grounded README sections that summarize measured reliability improvements, before-versus-after outputs, the refinement loop, and the current engineering stack.

operations.action: appended new README sections for controlled experiment results, before-versus-after examples, the iterative improvement loop, demonstrated capabilities, and the current tech stack using only persisted evaluation metrics and sample output artifacts
operations.reason: The documentation update required a readable, evidence-backed summary of system results and engineering capability without rewriting existing README content or introducing claims that were not supported by repository artifacts.

payload.description: Rewrote the README into a results-first portfolio document that leads with measured improvements, concrete examples, technical proof artifacts, and a compressed system overview.

operations.action: rewrote README.md using only current repository artifacts for metrics, examples, model-improvement claims, proof references, stack details, and the retained system overview and API summary
operations.reason: The documentation update required a recruiter-friendly portfolio readout that surfaces the strongest verified results first, preserves technical credibility, and removes low-signal bulk without changing any system behavior or inventing unsupported claims.

payload.description: Appended concise security, safety, and threat-model sections to the README to clarify current controls, trust boundaries, and known gaps without overstating production readiness.

operations.action: appended README sections for security and safety considerations, production considerations, trust boundaries, primary risks, current controls, known gaps, and a short safety note
operations.reason: The documentation update required a grounded statement of current safety posture and limits so the project signals production awareness without adding unsupported security or compliance claims.

payload.description: Added a lightweight pre-pipeline input validation gate that blocks obviously sensitive patterns at the API entry point and leaves downstream pipeline behavior unchanged.

operations.action: created service/input_validation.py with regex-based detection for email addresses, phone numbers, SSN-like values, date-of-birth formats, and long numeric identifiers, then inserted a minimal validation check in service/api.py before any run shell or pipeline execution begins
operations.reason: The service needed a small pre-processing guard that rejects clearly sensitive inputs before they enter the execution pipeline while avoiding any changes to graph nodes, governance logic, evaluation code, or downstream execution behavior.

payload.description: Validated the new API entry-point input filter in one pass by confirming that obvious sensitive input is rejected before execution and that safe input still follows the normal acceptance path without persisting test artifacts.

operations.action: performed one unsafe request check and one safe request check against the FastAPI evaluate endpoint with side-effect hooks patched, verified the unsafe path returned HTTP 400 with status rejected, verified the safe path returned an accepted run response, and confirmed the run store was unchanged during validation
operations.reason: The validation task required proving that the new regex-based guard is enforced at the API boundary before run creation while also confirming that safe requests still pass through the existing acceptance path without leaving persistent test data behind.

payload.description: Extended the README security section with a concise summary of the validated input filter behavior at the API boundary.

operations.action: added an Input Validation subsection under Security & Safety Considerations describing the regex-based sensitive-pattern checks, the verified rejected path for unsafe input, the accepted path for safe input, and two short concrete examples
operations.reason: The documentation update required a small extension to the existing safety section so the README reflects the verified behavior of the entry-point validation layer without rewriting the rest of the portfolio narrative.

payload.description: Prepared the repository for release by keeping internal SITREC notes local-only, staging the public artifacts, and packaging the final evaluated and governed system state for the v1.0 release.

operations.action: created a local-only post-phase SITREC note, excluded SITREC patterns and the local auth diagnostic from version control through .git/info/exclude, staged the intended public repository files, and finalized the release commit, tag, and push while keeping SITREC files out of tracking
operations.reason: The release step required a clean public repository state with the verified README, evaluation artifacts, fine-tuning artifacts, and service updates available on GitHub, while ensuring internal SITREC documents remained private and untracked.
